summary: Good matrix recording with track‑order glitches captures a legendary second set highlighted by an epic The Other One and a smoking St. Stephen jam.
review: The show is praised as one of the Dead’s greatest, especially the second half where Drums flows into a mesmerizing NFA stretch, a transcendent The Other One, and a joyful St. Stephen jam.  The matrix source sounds clean and beautiful to most listeners, though several reviewers note mis‑ordered and mislabeled tracks and occasional muffled mic quality.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong consensus on an outstanding performance (especially the second set) combined with a generally good matrix recording, tempered by consistent complaints about track order and occasional sound issues.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean and beautiful overall, but tracks are mis‑ordered/mislabeled and some listeners describe a muffled mic‑under‑pillow sound; matrix mix improves listenability despite these flaws.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as competent, while the second set is legendary—45 minutes from Drums through NFA are highlighted as a peak performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Early‑January laryngitis still present but his energy shines on the St. Stephen jam and other moments.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drums segment, especially its transition into NFA, is praised as one of the best continuous 45‑minute stretches.
